title: Codereview pass 3: MockRng SAFETY - defense-in-depth documentation is appropriate
description:
Location: rust/lichen-embassy/src/mock.rs:115-138, 146-151

The SAFETY comment addition satisfies the requirement from rust-review.md that every unsafe block have a SAFETY comment. Combined with the existing defense-in-depth:

1. Module-level doc warns 'DO NOT USE FOR CRYPTOGRAPHIC OPERATIONS'
2. Type-level doc explains deterministic seed and xorshift insecurity
3. #[deprecated] attribute fires in release builds
4. Feature flag separation (mock feature)

The new SAFETY comment completes the documentation chain. While the justification could be stronger (per project-LICHEN-ow1e.191), the overall approach of documenting test-only crypto mocks thoroughly is correct.

This is the right pattern: mock crypto implementations should be loud about their limitations at every level (module, type, function, unsafe block).
